title: SELECT ... FROM Distributed table returns multiple rows instead of single aggregated result
description:
### Company or project name
bitquery
### Describe what's wrong
Describe the bug
A query to a Distributed table that should return a single aggregated value sometimes returns multiple rows.
The problem appears presumably when one of the replicas is lagging behind.
If the query is executed on a non-lagging replica, the result is correct (single row).
SELECT hostName(), max(Block_Slot_Max)
FROM solana.dex_trades_price_minute_aggr
hostName()|max(Block_Slot_Max)|
----------+-------------------+
charch28  |361294653          |
charch30  |361294653          |
charch33  |361294653          |
charch34  |361084397          |
[multiple_rows_trace.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21897133/trace.txt)
[single_row_trace.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21897395/single_row_trace.txt)
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
ClickHouse version: 25.4.5.24
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
DDL:
CREATE TABLE solana.dex_trades_price_minute_aggr (
Block_Time DateTime,
Block_Date Date,
......
Block_Slot_Min SimpleAggregateFunction(min, UInt64),
Block_Slot_Max SimpleAggregateFunction(max, UInt64),
Partition_Id String,
Partition_Hash String
)
ENGINE = Distributed('streaming_solana_segment','solana','dex_trades_price_minute_aggr_storage');
SELECT cluster,shard_num,internal_replication,replica_num,host_name,is_local  FROM `system`.clusters AS c
WHERE `cluster` LIKE '%streaming_solana_segment%'
cluster                 |shard_num|internal_replication|replica_num|host_name|is_local|
------------------------+---------+--------------------+-----------+---------+--------+
streaming_solana_segment|        1|                   1|          1|charch28 |       0|
streaming_solana_segment|        1|                   1|          2|charch30 |       1|
streaming_solana_segment|        1|                   1|          3|charch33 |       0|
streaming_solana_segment|        1|                   1|          4|charch34 |       0|
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE solana.dex_trades_price_minute_aggr (Block_Time TIMESTAMP, Block_Date DATE, Block_Slot_Min BIGINT, Block_Slot_Max BIGINT, Partition_Id VARCHAR(255), Partition_Hash VARCHAR(255));

INSERT INTO solana.dex_trades_price_minute_aggr (Block_Time, Block_Date, Block_Slot_Min, Block_Slot_Max, Partition_Id, Partition_Hash) VALUES (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, CURRENT_DATE, 100, 200, 'partition_1', 'hash_1');

INSERT INTO solana.dex_trades_price_minute_aggr (Block_Time, Block_Date, Block_Slot_Min, Block_Slot_Max, Partition_Id, Partition_Hash) VALUES (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, CURRENT_DATE, 150, 250, 'partition_2', 'hash_2');
SELECT host_name(), MAX(Block_Slot_Max) FROM solana.dex_trades_price_minute_aggr GROUP BY host_name();
SELECT host_name(), MAX(Block_Slot_Max) FROM solana.dex_trades_price_minute_aggr WHERE Block_Slot_Max IS NOT NULL GROUP BY host_name();
key_bug_features: Querying Distributed table with lagging replica returns multiple rows
